name: Clerk API
what_it_does: Developer-friendly authentication and user management platform with drop-in UI components, a Backend API for user/org management, and first-class Next.js and React support.
use_cases: Adding auth to Next.js or React apps with minimal setup
Managing users and organizations via Backend API in agent workflows
Querying user sessions and metadata from backend agents
Syncing user data to databases via webhooks
not_for: Non-JavaScript ecosystems (limited backend SDK support outside Node)
Enterprise SAML SSO without paying for Pro plan
best_when: You're building a Next.js/React app and want authentication done in a day with a clean developer experience.
avoid_when: You need enterprise SAML/SCIM on a free plan or require a non-JS backend SDK.

auth.notes: Backend API uses a secret key (sk_live_...). Frontend uses publishable key. Simple and agent-friendly - no OAuth2 flow needed for backend operations.

pricing.notes: Generous free tier. Paid unlocks organizations, custom domains, advanced analytics, and enterprise SSO. MAU-based pricing.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; generous limits in practice

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Clear error codes with human-readable messages. Docs include error code reference.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: User creation can check for existing email. No explicit idempotency keys.
known_agent_gotchas: Backend API and Frontend API are different - agents use Backend API with secret key only
User metadata has two tiers: public (visible to frontend) and private (backend only) - easy to leak data to wrong tier
Session tokens expire quickly - backend must verify with Clerk on each request
Webhooks require svix signature verification - easy to skip and create security hole
Organization features are Pro-only - free apps can't use orgs
